perturbation.original_text: g. "Endorsed Product(s)" shall only mean and include Company's irons, woods, wedges and putters.
perturbation.changed_text: g. "Endorsed Product(s)" shall mean and include all of Company's products, including but not limited to, Company's irons, woods, wedges and putters. The company may, at its discretion, designate other products as 'Endorsed Products' with written notice to North.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of "Endorsed Product(s)" was limited to specific golf clubs. The modified version broadens this definition to include all of Company's products and grants the company the power to add more products to the definition at its discretion. This change introduces uncertainty about which products North is obligated to endorse and directly contradicts the exclusivity clause in Section 3, as North might inadvertently endorse products he didn't agree to. It also conflicts with Section 7g, regarding the supply of endorsed products, since the types and quantities will have no agreed limits.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the terms set forth in this Agreement, North hereby grants to Company the right and privilege to use North's Likeness and North's Endorsement during the Term and within the Contract Territory in all reasonable forms of advertising including, but not limited to television (including the infomercial format), radio, print advertising, brochures, pamphlets, product packaging, point-of-purchase materials, Company's web-site and a demonstration video (hereinafter referred to as "Advertising Materials") in connection with Company's advertisement and sale of the Endorsed Product only. During the Term of this Agreement and within the Contract Territory, North agrees not to enter into an agreement with another company or entity for the purpose of endorsing or promoting products similar to the Endorsed Products. Upon the expiration or termination of this Agreement, Company shall be prohibited from using or distributing any new or existing Advertising Materials using North's Likeness and North's Endorsement except for Endorsed Product with North's Likeness and/or North's Endorsement on the packaging that is already in the channel of retail distribution. Company shall be prohibited from selling or shipping any new Endorsed Product with North's Likeness and/or North's Endorsement on the packaging upon the expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly broadens the definition of "Endorsed Product(s)" from specific golf clubs to all of Company's products and allows for future additions at the company's discretion. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which limits North's endorsement and promotional activities to the specifically defined "Endorsed Product only." The changed text introduces uncertainty and conflicts with North's exclusivity agreement, as he could inadvertently endorse a wider range of products, breaking the terms set forth in the agreement. This creates a strong in-text contradiction regarding the scope of North's endorsement obligations.

perturbation.original_text: All materials, in any form whatsoever, relating to the Advertising Materials will be and shall remain the absolute property of Company. North acknowledge that they do not now have and in the future will assert no right, title, or interest of any kind or nature whatsoever, in or to any component of the Advertising Materials.
perturbation.changed_text: All materials, in any form whatsoever, relating to the Advertising Materials will be and shall remain the absolute property of Company, unless North contributed creative input. North acknowledges that they do not now have and in the future will assert no right, title, or interest of any kind or nature whatsoever, in or to any component of the Advertising Materials, except for elements directly resulting from North's unique performance or likeness that are separable from the overall work.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that all advertising materials are the sole property of the Company, and North has no claim. The modification adds an exception, stating that if North contributes creative input or elements directly resulting from his performance, he may retain rights to those separable elements. This contradicts the initial statement of absolute ownership by the Company, creating uncertainty regarding intellectual property rights. This has implications to the indemnification clause in Section 15, because it is now uncear who owns what in the advertising material.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Company shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless North and its employees, officers, agents, and representatives from and against any and all claims, suits, assessments, losses, obligations, penalties, charges, actions, damages, liabilities, costs, and expenses including reasonable attorney's fees whether incurred at trial or in connection with any review by appeal or certiorari of both the trial and appellate court levels (collectively referred to as "Claims") arising out of or in connection with: a Any claim or action for negligent or intentional misconduct of Company in relation to the advertisement, promotion, or sale of the Endorsed Product, or Company's use of North's Likeness or North's Endorsement to advertise, promote, distribute, or sell the Endorsed Product; or b. Any claim or action for personal injury, death, or property damage resulting from a customer's use of the Endorsed Product; or c. Any claims or action for the breach by Company of any of its representations and warranties set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text asserts the Company's absolute ownership of all advertising materials, while the changed text introduces an exception granting North potential rights based on creative contributions. This directly contradicts the original statement, creating uncertainty about who owns what in the advertising material, and therefore impacting the indemnification clause in Section 15, as it is unclear who owns what in the advertising material. This uncertainty directly undermines the indemnification clause, creating a strong contradiction as the party responsible for indemnification related to intellectual property claims is no longer definitively established.

perturbation.original_text: Amounts not received within ten (10) days following their respective due dates shall be assessed a late charge equal to one and one-half percent (1 _%) per month of the overdue amount until paid, commencing on the eleventh day.
perturbation.changed_text: Amounts not received within ten (10) days following their respective due dates may, at the Company's sole discretion, be assessed a late charge equal to one and one-half percent (1 _%) per month of the overdue amount until paid, commencing on the eleventh day. However, the Company reserves the right to waive late payment fees in cases of demonstrated financial hardship by the Company.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that late payments *shall* be assessed a late charge. The modified version makes the assessment of late charges discretionary ("may, at the Company's sole discretion") and allows the company to waive the fee entirely. This creates an in-text contradiction because the initial part of the changed sentence implies a potential charge while the added sentence completely undermines that certainty, leaving the payment schedule in Section 7.a open to subjective interpretation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration for the rights, services and benefits granted by North hereunder, Company agrees to pay North a non-refundable Guaranteed Service Fee and Marketing Retainer (hereinafter referred to as "Guaranteed Fee") of fifty-five thousand dollars ($55,000USD) in Contract Year 2011 and seventy thousand dollars ($70,000USD) in Contract Year 2012. (i) Twenty-Five thousand dollars ($25,000USD) on or before two days after this Agreement is fully executed. (ii) Fifteen thousand dollars ($15,000USD) on or before July 1, 2011. (iii) Fifteen thousand dollars ($ 15,000USD) on or before November 1, 2011. (i) Twenty-Five thousand dollars ($25,000USD) on or before February 1, 2012 (ii) Twenty-Five thousand dollars ($25,000USD) on or before July 1, 2012 (iii) Twenty thousand dollars ($20,000USD) on or before November 1, 2012
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated late charges ('shall'). The changed text makes them discretionary ('may') and waivable. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which outlines a fixed payment schedule ('agrees to pay') with specific due dates, implying mandatory payments without any possibility of waived late fees. The changed text introduces uncertainty and potential deviation from this schedule, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
